Common.disclaimer: Unofficial — fan-made. Not affiliated with Nugem / Tesseract Studio. All game names and assets belong to their owners. Data is datamined and may change with patches.

Home.title: TBH Trade
Home.tagline: Combines the probabilities of Synthesis and Craft operations with Steam Market prices to answer “is this operation profitable?”
Home.ladderNote: {count} grades. Only {minGrade} and above are tradable on Steam; below that, market value is $0.

Synthesis.title: Synthesis Calculator
Synthesis.description: Synthesize 9 same-grade items → grade distribution, expected value and profit chance. From Immortal onward there is a {fail} (same grade) risk.
Synthesis.metaDescription: Calculates the probability, expected value (EV), ROI, fail and profit chance of TBH: Task Bar Hero synthesis operations using Steam Market prices.
Synthesis.inputAutoHint: Cheapest of {count} market items: {price}. A rational player buys the cheapest ×9.
Synthesis.costNoteFarm: Input not tradable → cost 0 (farm mode); net = expected sell revenue.
Synthesis.footer: Revenue is after fee (Steam 15%). You pay the listing price when buying the input; the fee is deducted when selling the output. Only Legendary+ grades are tradable; below that, $0.

Craft.title: Craft Calculator
Craft.description: Compare recipe materials against the grade-distributed output → cost, expected value and profit chance. There is no fail in crafting.
Craft.metaDescription: Calculates the material cost, expected value (EV), ROI and profit chance of TBH: Task Bar Hero craft recipes using Steam Market prices.
Craft.costNote: Cost = sum of the cheapest listing prices of the recipe materials. Crafting has no fail.
Craft.footer: Revenue is after fee (Steam 15%). You pay the listing price when buying materials. Only Legendary+ grades are tradable; below that, $0.

Opportunities.title: Best Opportunities
Opportunities.description: Feasible scenarios ranked by ROI, computed from cached Steam prices. Refreshed after each price update.
Opportunities.metaDescription: ROI-ranked list of the most profitable TBH: Task Bar Hero synthesis and craft operations, based on Steam Market prices.
Opportunities.empty: No opportunities computed yet. Run a price ingest + scan.

Detail.notListed: Not listed on the Steam Market.
Detail.notTradable: This grade is not tradable (market value $0).
Detail.cooldownNote: Recently updated — try again in a few minutes

Methodology.title: Methodology
Methodology.metaDescription: How TBH Trade computes profitability: odds sources, price freshness, Steam fee and the buy/sell asymmetry.
Methodology.intro: How TBH Trade computes “is this profitable?”.
Methodology.oddsBody: Synthesis and craft odds are datamined from taskbarhero.wiki and normalized to 1 (raw datamined rates are not always exactly 100%). They are versioned and may change with patches.
Methodology.priceBody: All prices come from the Steam Community Market cache. The frontend never contacts Steam directly; a server-side worker collects prices on a schedule. Buy cost uses the lowest listing; sell value uses the median when volume is sufficient, otherwise the lowest. Listings below $0.03 are treated as ghost/scam and ignored. For synthesis, the per-input cost defaults to the cheapest item of that grade and tier (a rational player buys the cheapest ×9) — you can override it manually.
Methodology.feeBody: You pay the listing price when buying inputs/materials. When selling the output, a 15% Steam fee is deducted (revenue = price × 0.85).
Methodology.tradableBody: Only Legendary and above are tradable on the Steam Market; lower grades have a $0 market value (their alchemy gold value is shown separately).
Methodology.failBody: From Immortal onward, synthesis can fail (the item stays at the same grade = value loss). Expected value and fail probability reflect this.

Status.title: System Status
Status.metaDescription: Price ingest health, data coverage and the latest worker runs for TBH Trade.
Status.description: Worker health, price freshness and data coverage.
Status.noRuns: No runs recorded yet. Trigger /api/ingest or run pnpm ingest:once.
